rationale: Gov't Code §1204.006 governs maximum interest rates for public securities. It has no relation to homestead exemption amounts or property tax appraisal under Tax Code §11.13(b).
quote: The maximum rate of interest for any issue or series of public securities, including an issue or series that is issued in exchange for property, labor, services, materials, or equipment under another law, is a net effective interest rate of 15 percent.
_note: No conflict. The cited rule addresses public securities interest rate ceilings, not property tax exemptions.
